title: 10 best herbal teas for sunburn
intro_desc: Herbal teas for sunburn are infusions made from plants that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and redness caused by sunburn. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for sunburn include chamomile, calendula, and lavender. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling, while calendula tea has been shown to promote skin healing and lavender tea has a calming effect that can help soothe irritated skin. By using these herbal teas as a compress or adding them to a bath, individuals can find relief from the pain and discomfort of sunburn, allowing them to enjoy their summer activities without worrying about the negative effects of sun exposure.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera teas helps with sunburn because of its exceptional soothing and cooling properties. The gel extracted from aloe vera leaves contains anti-inflammatory compounds that quickly calm red, itchy skin and reduce swelling. Drinking aloe vera tea also provides antioxidant-rich vitamins A, C, and E to the body, which help neutralize free radicals and promote collagen production. This combination of internal and external benefits accelerates the healing process, reducing discomfort and promoting a faster recovery from sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe Vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe Vera's antioxidants neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting healing.
Wound-healing properties: Aloe Vera's polysaccharides and glycoproteins accelerate wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue regeneration, and enhancing immune function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their rich concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Leaves: The leaves contain gel-like substance with potent anti-irritant and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried aloe vera leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes to allow flavors to blend.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Add honey and lemon juice to taste with one teaspoon of each ingredient recommended.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and enjoying within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with sunburn because it contains flavonoids and polyphenols that have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburned skin. The tea's antioxidants also work to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, promoting the repair of damaged skin cells and reducing peeling and itching. Additionally, the soothing properties of pot marigold may help calm discomfort and irritation, providing relief from sunburned skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Pot marigold tea's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, which can damage skin cells and lead to inflammation and sunburn.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Wound-Healing Activity: Pot marigold's wound-healing activity is thought to be due to its ability to stimulate collagen synthesis, improve blood vessel formation, and enhance skin hydration, all of which can help accelerate the recovery process from sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin.
Seeds: Containing high amounts of omega-3 fatty acids, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers for the tea recipe.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried calendula flowers or two tablespoons of fresh petals for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured flowers and water in a medium-sized saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the flavors to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving the tea hot or cold.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with sunburn because it possesses calming and soothing properties that can effectively reduce inflammation, redness, and itching associated with sunburned skin. The tea's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds help to alleviate discomfort and promote healing by reducing swelling and promoting the regeneration of new skin cells. Additionally, the tea's relaxing qualities can also help to calm the mind and body, further aiding in the recovery process from a sunburn.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender tea's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes in lavender tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and discomfort.
Calming and soothing properties: Lavender's calming effect on the nervous system can help reduce anxiety, stress, and itchiness associated with sunburn, promoting relaxation and ease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Bark: The bark contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in sunburned areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavender flowers of lavandula angustifolia for 30g.
Combine harvested lavender flowers with boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 7-10 minutes allowing the essential oils to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Serve the tea hot or warm, sweetened with honey if desired, within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint teas helps with sunburn because of its soothing properties that calm irritated skin. The tea's natural anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn. Additionally, spearmint tea's astringent properties help to tighten and cool the skin, providing relief from heat and discomfort. As a result, it can be a natural remedy for alleviating sunburn symptoms, promoting a faster recovery, and leaving skin feeling calm and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Spearmint tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV rays and reduce inflammation, promoting healing and soothing sunburned skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in spearmint tea, such as rosmarinic acid, can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn, while also accelerating the healing process.

Cooling and soothing properties: Spearmint tea's cooling and soothing effects can provide immediate relief from sunburn pain, itching, and heat, making it a comforting and calming remedy for sun-kissed skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, spearmint roots help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Leaves: Containing rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, spearmint leaves provide a cooling and refreshing effect to reduce redness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried spearmint leaves from your garden or store in airtight containers for up to six months.
Measure out one tablespoon of fresh or two teaspoons of dried spearmint leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat filtered or bottled water to a rolling boil and pour it over the measured spearmint leaves in a large teapot or mug.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes or until the desired strength and flavor are achieved.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and enjoy warm or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with sunburn because of its soothing and calming properties. The tea's flavonoids, such as caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce redness and swelling caused by sun exposure. Additionally, lemon balm's antiseptic and antioxidant compounds help to promote skin healing and protect against further irritation. As a result, drinking lemon balm tea can provide relief from sunburn discomfort, itching, and dryness, making it a natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea's antioxidants help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sunburns.
Sobering and calming effects: Lemon balm's soothing properties can calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by sunburn, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemon balm roots can help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Leaves: Containing high amounts of vitamin C and flavonoids, lemon balm leaves are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves or purchase dried herbal tea.
Rinse the harvested or purchased herbal material with filtered water.
Measure and combine the herbal material with boiling water.
Steep the herbal mixture for 5-7 minutes to release the flavors and aromas.
Strain and serve the herbal tea in a warm or iced beverage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling caused by sun exposure. The antioxidants present in sage tea also work to combat free radicals that can damage skin cells, promoting a faster healing process. Additionally, the cooling effects of sage tea can provide instant relief from burning sensation, making it a soothing and natural remedy for sunburned skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Sage tea contains high levels of antioxidant compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation and reduce oxidative stress on the skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds in sage tea have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe sunburned skin, reducing redness, swelling, and pain.

Antiseptic and antimicrobial properties: Sage tea's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties can help prevent bacterial and fungal infections often associated with sunburns, promoting a faster healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Bark: Containing tannins and flavonoids, sage bark reduces inflammation and itching caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in late summer or early fall.
Measure one teaspoon of dried sage leaves or two teaspoons of fresh sage leaves per cup.
Bring filtered water to a boil and pour it over the measured sage leaves in a teapot.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, before serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with sunburn because of its exceptional soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids work together to reduce redness and swelling, calming irritated skin. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial compounds help prevent infection and promote healthy wound healing. As a natural remedy, herbal thyme teas can provide quick relief from sunburn discomfort, helping to restore the skin's natural balance and reducing peeling time.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with sunburned skin.
Wound-healing properties: Thyme tea's antibacterial and antifungal properties promote wound healing by preventing infection and promoting tissue repair, which can aid in the recovery from sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers possess analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and discomfort caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried thymus vulgaris leaves in small quantities for brewing.
Measure one teaspoon of dried thyme per cup of boiling water.
Steep the thyme leaves in hot water for five to seven minutes, depending on strength desired.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and consuming immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet basil teas helps with sunburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm irritated skin. The antioxidants present in sweet basil also help to reduce redness and swelling caused by sun exposure. Additionally, the tea's cooling effects can provide instant relief from burning sensations and discomfort, while its hydrating properties help to replenish moisture lost due to excessive sweating or dehydration. Overall, herbal sweet basil teas offer a gentle and effective way to alleviate sunburned skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal sweet basil tea helps to combat sun damage by neutralizing free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and promoting skin repair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sweet basil tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as eugenol and citronellol, help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sunburn, providing relief and promoting healing.

Softp antimicrobial properties: Basil's essential oils have been shown to exhibit soft antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi that can cause skin infections or worsen sunburn symptoms, helping to prevent opportunistic infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots are used to soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, the leaves help to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote healing of sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sweet basil leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine one tablespoon of dried basil or two tablespoons of fresh basil with 8 oz boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse and meld together nicely.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Serve the tea hot or let it cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with sunburn because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin. The antioxidants present in rosemary tea help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, its antiseptic properties prevent infection and promote healthy healing by reducing inflammation and repairing damaged skin cells. Drinking rosemary tea can also help to rehydrate the body and replenish lost electrolytes, further aiding in the recovery process from sunburn.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Rosemary tea's antioxidants help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by sun damage, promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications like blisters and peeling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds in rosemary tea, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, making it a natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin.

Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary tea's antimicrobial properties help to prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can occur when the skin is damaged by sunburn, reducing the risk of further complications like cellulitis or sepsis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin.
Leaves: High in vitamins A and C, which help to regenerate damaged skin cells and reduce redness and inflammation associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosemary leaves from your garden or store-bought packets in amounts of one teaspoon per cup.
Rinse the harvested rosemary leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or debris then chop them finely.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped rosemary leaves with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser for steeping times ranging from five to ten minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug or teapot removing the solids and discarding the leaves.
Sweeten the tea to taste by adding honey or sugar stir well and serve hot or chill in refrigerator for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley teas helps with sunburn because its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's flavonoids and beta-carotene work to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort, while its cooling effect helps to ease the stinging sensation often associated with sunburn. Additionally, parsley's natural astringent qualities help to dry out blisters and prevent infection, promoting healthy healing and recovery from sun damage.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Parsley tea contains antioxidants such as vitamin C, beta-carotene, and flavonoids, which help neutralize free radicals formed by UV radiation, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols and omega-3 fatty acids in parsley tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Soil-replenishing properties: Parsley is a natural diuretic and has been used to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne. Drinking parsley tea after sunburn may help replenish the skin's natural moisture barrier and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Seeds: Containing oil-rich fatty acids and flavonoids, these seeds help soothe and calm irritated skin while promoting wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Rinse fresh parsley leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried parsley flakes or two tablespoons of fresh parsley leaves.
Bring one cup of water to boil in a medium-sized pot for five minutes.
Steep the parsley in boiling water for ten to fifteen minutes before straining.
Pour the tea into a cup and serve immediately.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sunburn with herbal teas is by using cooled tea bags directly on the affected area. Chamomile and calendula teas are ideal for soothing sunburns due to their anti-inflammatory properties. Steep several tea bags in boiling water, let it cool, and then gently apply the tea bags to the sunburn. For a more potent remedy, create a strong tea by using a larger quantity of tea leaves and follow the same application process. Repeat this treatment several times a day for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sunburn that are treated with herbal teas include sun poisoning, windburn, and rosacea. Chamomile tea is often used as a compress to soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation. Calendula tea is also known for its healing properties and can be used to treat minor burns, cuts, and skin irritations. For windburn, lavender tea can be applied topically to moisturize and repair damaged skin. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, can help reduce redness and accelerate skin healing. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas for medicinal purposes, especially for severe or persistent skin conditions.
